title: You Don't Need React Portals For Modals Anymore (react-call)
summary: react-call is a lightweight React utility that enables calling components as asynchronous functions, replacing the need for React Portals when building modals. It uses Root and Stack, Upsert Singletons, and Mutation Flow patterns to handle layout isolation and state management without context providers or complex state tracking.
writeup: This video introduces react-call, a lightweight React utility that lets components be called as asynchronous functions, presenting it as an alternative to React Portals for building modals. It matters because the usual modal approach relies on context providers and manual state tracking, which react-call avoids. The concrete takeaways: the library uses Root and Stack along with Upsert Singletons and a Mutation Flow pattern to handle layout isolation and state management, removing the need for context providers and complex state tracking.
